note: twig_vardumper is a tiny, config-less developer/theming module: one Twig extension (Drupal\twig_vardumper\TwigExtension) exposing two variadic functions, dump() and vardumper(), both backed by Symfony\Component\VarDumper\VarDumper::dump(). It ships no admin UI, no config entities, no settings, no permissions, and no Drush commands, so all three tiers are expressed against its only surface — the Twig runtime. EASY (recipe) covers the function names, the Twig-debug requirement, and the symfony/var-dumper dependency. MEDIUM (introspection): the module owns no saved config value to read back, so — exactly as with the cva module — the KNOWN state is the module being enabled and its Twig extension being live; the agent must inspect the running container (the twig service's registered extension and its functions) to answer. This is retained (not omitted) because the grading target is discovered from live runtime state, not recited config. HARD (execution, drupal-expert): the agent authors a real Twig template that calls dump()/vardumper() with known values; verify renders it live with Twig debug forced on IN-PROCESS (the module returns FALSE unless $env->isDebug()) and greps that the rendered Symfony VarDumper markup (sf-dump) contains the dumped value(s). The reset removes the template (empty state => verify FAILs); enabling debug via enableDebug() is in-process only, so no global twig.config/cache state is mutated and the site is left clean.

evals.prompt: In Drupal, which Twig function(s) does the Twig VarDumper (twig_vardumper) module make available in templates for debugging variables?
I added {{ dump(node) }} using the twig_vardumper module to a template but nothing is printed. What condition must be met for twig_vardumper's dump()/vardumper() functions to actually produce output?
Which PHP/Symfony library does the twig_vardumper module rely on to render its pretty variable dumps?
On this live Drupal site, twig_vardumper is enabled. Inspect the running site (e.g. with drush) and report the names of the Twig functions its Twig extension registers on the active twig service.

This site uses twig_vardumper. Inspect the running site and report the fully-qualified class name of the Twig extension that twig_vardumper registers on the twig service (the class providing the dump/vardumper functions).

Using the twig_vardumper module's dump() Twig function, create a Twig template at web/sites/default/files/twig_vardumper-eval/dump.html.twig that dumps the string 'TVD-SCALAR-9021'. The evaluation renders this template with Twig debug enabled and confirms the Symfony VarDumper output contains that value. Do not hand-write the value as plain markup — it must be produced by dump().

Using the twig_vardumper module's vardumper() Twig function, create a Twig template at web/sites/default/files/twig_vardumper-eval/vardumper.html.twig that dumps an array containing exactly the two strings 'TVD-ALPHA' and 'TVD-BETA'. The evaluation renders this template with Twig debug enabled and confirms the Symfony VarDumper output contains both values. The output must be produced by vardumper(), not written as plain markup.
